title: Is the zero-divisor graph Gamma(Z_47 x Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: strong moved-barrier continuation: the same B={1,43,47,53} used beyond the frozen-wrapper boundary still works at p=47 and leaves a complementary prime pool with slack 2
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_47 × Z_25) survives skeptical verification. This is still an instance result, but it materially strengthens the active Γ(Z_p × Z_25) campaign because the same moved sparse barrier B = {1,43,47,53} already preserved at p = 37, 41, 43 still works at p = 47 and leaves a complementary prime pool with slack 2.
candidate_theorem_slice: If {1,...,5p+19} admits a pairwise-coprime moved barrier B = {1,q1,q2,q3} whose nontrivial multiples fit inside the 20 labels of A and A union B can be kept inside a controlled excluded factor palette that still leaves at least p - 1 primes for C, then Γ(Z_p × Z_25) is prime.
next_action: promote the verified p = 47 moved-barrier witness into the family dossier, run generalize before any Lean work on the exact instance, and then test z53-z25-prime-zero-divisor-graph
